description_long: This is a pair of vintage-style scissors featuring two straight, narrow metal blades joined at a central pivot. The handles are cylindrical and made of wood, showing a natural brown grain and a smooth finish, each attached to the metal arms with visible fasteners. The blades are black, flat, and rectangular, tapering to a slightly pointed tip, and appear to be made of steel or iron. The pivot joint is a simple, exposed rivet, allowing the blades to open and close smoothly. The overall design is utilitarian, with no finger loops, suggesting these are specialized shears, possibly for industrial or craft use. There are no visible brand markings or decorative elements, emphasizing function over form.
description: A pair of vintage-style scissors with straight black metal blades and smooth cylindrical wooden handles, joined by a simple rivet. The design is utilitarian, lacking finger loops and decorative features.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the scissors, highlighting the straight black blades and the cylindrical wooden handles.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the narrow profile of the blades and the symmetrical spread of the wooden handles.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the straight alignment of the blades and the handles, with the pivot joint visible.
description_view_3: Top-down view emphasizing the parallel arrangement of the handles and the flat, rectangular shape of the blades.
attribute_handle_type: Cylindrical, symmetrical, smooth wooden handles; ambidextrous and not padded or coated.
attribute_blade_joint_type: Simple exposed rivet joint; not adjustable, visible at the intersection of the blades.
attribute_finger_loop_design: No finger loops; handles are straight and cylindrical, designed for gripping rather than finger insertion.
attribute_blade_tip_style: Slightly pointed, straight blade tips; designed for precision cutting.
